self_report.correct_answer: ((2n-1)/e)^{(2n-1)/2} < 1·3·5···(2n-1) < ((2n+1)/e)^{(2n+1)/2} holds for all positive integers n.
self_report.reflection: Taking logs and using f(x) = (x/2)(ln x - 1) with f'(x) = (ln x)/2, the claim is equivalent to f(2n-1) < sum_{k=1}^n ln(2k-1) < f(2n+1). Induction works: at each step we add ln(2n+1) to the sum, while f(2n+3)-f(2n+1) > ln(2n+1) > f(2n+1)-f(2n-1) because ln is strictly increasing on the integration intervals. Base n=1 and spot checks at n=2,3 hold with margin.
self_report.notes: Standard Putnam 1996 B2 statement; the integral-comparison induction with f(x)=(x/2)(ln x - 1) gives the bracketing exactly.
